action.reasoning: Observation: The previous run failed with a segfault (exit code 139) exactly when importing `torch_geometric.data`. Analysis: The pre-installed `torch_geometric` or one of its C++ extensions (like `torch_sparse` or `torch_scatter`) is likely compiled for a different PyTorch version or CUDA version, causing a segfault upon import. Decision: I will completely bypass `torch_geometric` and implement a custom GIN (Graph Isomorphism Network) model using standard PyTorch operations (`torch.scatter_add_`). I will also write a custom `DataLoader` `collate_fn` to batch the graphs. This will guarantee that no segfaults occur from broken C++ extensions. I will train for 2 epochs and submit an evaluation to get a baseline score.
